 {"id":"gt-4nn.2","title":"Molecule instantiation: create child beads from template","description":"When instantiating a molecule on a work bead:

## Transaction Flow

1. Parse molecule's `## Step:` sections from description
2. Begin SQLite transaction
3. For each step, create child issue:
   - ID: `{parent-id}.{step-ref}` or generated
   - Title: step title (from header or first line)
   - Description: step prose instructions
   - Type: task
   - Priority: inherit from parent
4. Add `instantiated-from` edge from each step to molecule:
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata)
   VALUES (step_id, mol_id, 'instantiated-from', '{"step": "implement"}');
   ```
5. Wire inter-step dependencies from `Needs:` lines
6. Commit transaction (atomic - all or nothing)

## Parsing Conventions

```markdown
## Step: <ref>
<prose instructions>
Needs: <step>, <step>  # optional
Tier: haiku|sonnet|opus  # optional hint
```

## Parameterization

Steps can have `{{variable}}` placeholders:
```markdown
## Step: implement
Implement {{feature_name}} in {{target_file}}.
```

Context map provided at instantiation time.

## API

```go
func (s *Store) InstantiateMolecule(mol *Issue, parent *Issue, ctx map[string]string) ([]*Issue, error)
func ParseMoleculeSteps(description string) ([]MoleculeStep, error)
```

Implementation lives in `internal/beads/molecule.go`.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.071066-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.028198-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.028198-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:52.072554-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:02.949242-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4nn.3","title":"Molecule CLI: bd molecule commands","description":"Add molecule commands to bd:

## Commands

```bash
bd molecule list              # List molecules (type: molecule)
bd molecule show <id>         # Show molecule with parsed steps
bd molecule parse <id>        # Validate and show parsed structure  
bd molecule instantiate <mol-id> --parent=<issue-id>  # Create steps
bd molecule instances <mol-id>  # Show all instantiations
```

## gt spawn integration

```bash
gt spawn --issue <id> --molecule <mol-id>
```

This should:
1. Call `bd molecule instantiate` (creates child beads atomically)
2. Spawn polecat on first ready step
3. Polecat grinds through via `bd ready`

## Output Examples

```
$ bd molecule show mol-abc

mol-abc: Engineer in a Box
Type: molecule

Steps (5):
  design      → (ready first)
  implement   → Needs: design
  review      → Needs: implement
  test        → Needs: implement  
  submit      → Needs: review, test
  
Instances: 3
```

```
$ bd molecule instances mol-abc

Parent          Status    Created
gt-xyz          done      2025-12-15
gt-abc          active    2025-12-17 (3/5 complete)
gt-def          pending   2025-12-18
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:53.919884-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:37.165927-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:00:37.165927-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:06:53.921621-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:03.048941-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4nn.4","title":"Built-in molecules: engineer-in-box, quick-fix, research","description":"Create built-in molecules as Beads issues:

## engineer-in-box

```markdown
id: mol-engineer-in-box
type: molecule
title: Engineer in a Box

Full workflow from design to merge.

## Step: design
Think carefully about architecture. Consider:
- Existing patterns in the codebase
- Trade-offs between approaches  
- Testability and maintainability

Write a brief design summary before proceeding.

## Step: implement
Write the code. Follow codebase conventions.
Needs: design

## Step: review
Self-review the changes. Look for:
- Bugs and edge cases
- Style issues
- Missing error handling
Needs: implement

## Step: test
Write and run tests. Cover happy path and edge cases.
Fix any failures before proceeding.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge via refinery.
Needs: review, test
```

## quick-fix

```markdown
id: mol-quick-fix
type: molecule  
title: Quick Fix

Fast path for small changes.

## Step: implement
Make the fix. Keep it focused.

## Step: test
Run relevant tests. Fix any regressions.
Needs: implement

## Step: submit
Submit for merge.
Needs: test
```

## research

```markdown
id: mol-research
type: molecule
title: Research

Investigation workflow.

## Step: investigate
Explore the question. Search code, read docs, 
understand context. Take notes.

## Step: document
Write up findings. Include:
- What you learned
- Recommendations
- Open questions
Needs: investigate
```

## Storage

Built-in molecules live in `<town>/.beads/` as regular issues.
Created during `gt install` or `bd init`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.574565-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:02:19.332406-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:02:19.332406-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4nn.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4nn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:07:04.576587-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-53w6","title":"Witness MVP: Automated Polecat Lifecycle","description":"Implement the Witness agent - per-rig 'pit boss' that manages polecat lifecycles.

The Witness enables hands-free swarming by automating:
- Spawning polecats for ready work
- Monitoring worker health
- Processing shutdown requests  
- Cleaning up worktrees when done
- Escalating stuck workers to Mayor

Without Witness, humans must manually spawn, monitor, and kill each polecat.

## Core Loop

```
while True:
  # Handle pending shutdowns
  for polecat in polecats where state == pending_shutdown:
    verify git clean
    kill session  
    remove worktree
    delete branch
    
  # Spawn for ready work
  ready = bd ready --parent=<epic> if epic else bd ready
  for issue in ready:
    if active_workers < max_workers:
      gt spawn --issue <id>
      
  # Check worker health
  for polecat in active polecats:
    if stuck (no progress for 30 min):
      nudge or escalate
      
  sleep 60
```

## Blocking Bugs to Fix
- gt-dsfi: handoff deadlock
- gt-n7z7: refinery foreground race condition
- gm-c6b: mail coordination (town vs rig beads)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-20T03:13:45.075731-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T09:32:16.49265-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T09:32:16.49265-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-83k0","title":"mol-witness-patrol molecule definition","description":"Create mol-witness-patrol in builtin_molecules.go.

## Steps (10 total)
1. inbox-check - Process witness mail (lifecycle, help requests)
2. load-state - Read handoff bead, get nudge counts
3. survey-workers - gt polecat list, categorize by status
4. inspect-workers - tmux capture-pane for each 'working' polecat
5. decide-actions - Apply nudge matrix, queue actions
6. execute-actions - Nudge, kill, or escalate as decided
7. save-state - Update handoff bead with new states
8. generate-summary - Summarize cycle for digest
9. context-check - Check own context usage
10. burn-or-loop - Squash wisp, then loop or cycle session

## Key Behaviors
- Uses wisp storage (.beads-wisp/)
- Reads/writes witness handoff bead for state persistence
- Progressive nudging (3 levels before escalate)
- Pre-kill verification before killing polecats

## Reference
- See prompts/roles/witness.md for protocol details
- See mol-refinery-patrol for similar structure

+{"id":"gt-87jz","title":"mol-witness-patrol","description":"Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop using the Christmas Ornament pattern.

The Witness is the Pit Boss for your rig. You watch polecats, nudge them toward
completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## The Christmas Ornament Shape

This molecule uses dynamic bonding to create inspection arms per-polecat:

```
                    ★ mol-witness-patrol (trunk)
                   /|\
            ┌─────┘ │ └─────┐
         PREFLIGHT DISCOVERY CLEANUP
            │       │        │
        ┌───┴───┐ ┌─┴─┐  ┌───┴───┐
        │inbox  │ │sur│  │aggreg │
        │refnry │ │vey│  │save   │
        │load   │ └─┬─┘  │summary│
        └───────┘   │    │contxt │
                    │    │loop   │
          ┌─────────┼─────────┐ └───────┘
          │         │         │
          ●         ●         ●     mol-polecat-arm (dynamic)
         ace       nux      toast
          │         │         │
       ┌──┴──┐   ┌──┴──┐   ┌──┴──┐
       │cap  │   │cap  │   │cap  │
       │ass  │   │ass  │   │ass  │
       │dec  │   │dec  │   │dec  │
       │exec │   │exec │   │exec │
       └──┬──┘   └──┬──┘   └──┬──┘
          │         │         │
          └─────────┴─────────┘
                    │
                 ⬣ base (cleanup)
```

## Phases

### PREFLIGHT (fixed steps)
1. inbox-check - Process lifecycle requests, help messages
2. check-refinery - Ensure MQ is alive and processing
3. load-state - Read persistent state (nudge counts, etc.)

### DISCOVERY (spawns dynamic arms)
4. survey-workers - List polecats, bond mol-polecat-arm per polecat
5. run-plugins - Bond mol-plugin-runner for each witness plugin

### CLEANUP (gate + fixed steps)
6. aggregate - GATE: WaitsFor all arms + plugins to complete
7. save-state - Persist nudge counts, action log
8. generate-summary - Create digest content
9. context-check - Check if context is high
10. burn-or-loop - Squash/burn wisp, then loop or exit

## Dynamic Arms

Each polecat gets mol-polecat-arm bonded as a wisp child:
- capture - Capture tmux output
- assess - Categorize state (working/idle/error/done)
- load-history - Get nudge counts for this polecat
- decide - Apply nudge matrix
- execute - Take action (nudge/kill/escalate/none)

Arms run in PARALLEL. The aggregate step waits for all to complete.

## Activity Feed

This design enables real-time visibility:

```
[14:32:08] + patrol-x7k.arm-ace bonded (5 steps)
[14:32:09] → patrol-x7k.arm-ace.capture in_progress
[14:32:10] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace.capture completed
[14:32:14] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace.decide completed (action: nudge-1)
[14:32:17] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace COMPLETE
```

## Storage

- Wisp storage: .beads-wisp/ (ephemeral, gitignored)
- Persistent state: witness handoff bead (nudge counts, etc.)
- Digests: Squashed summaries in permanent beads

## Dependencies

- bd-xo1o: Dynamic Molecule Bonding epic (in beads rig)
  - bd mol bond with variable substitution
  - WaitsFor directive for fanout gates
  - Activity feed query

 {"id":"gt-8bx","title":"Adaptive backoff for daemon heartbeat","description":"Track agent responsiveness and adjust heartbeat frequency.

## Per-Agent State

```go
type AgentBackoff struct {
    BaseInterval    time.Duration  // 60s default
    CurrentInterval time.Duration  // grows when busy
    MaxInterval     time.Duration  // 10min cap
    ConsecutiveMiss int            // pokes with no response
}
```

## Strategy Options

- **Fixed**: Always 60s (current, simple)
- **Geometric**: 60s → 90s → 135s → 202s (factor 1.5)
- **Exponential**: 60s → 120s → 240s (factor 2, aggressive)

## Recovery

When agent responds (runs a command):
- Reset ConsecutiveMiss to 0
- Return to BaseInterval immediately

## Benefits

- Reduces noise for busy agents
- Saves resources during quiet periods
- Still catches stuck agents (max interval cap)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:33.083844-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:09:31.613912-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:09:31.613912-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8bx","depends_on_id":"gt-bfd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.912289-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8dry","title":"Add role-specific subcommands that delegate to core commands (agent UX)","description":"## Problem

Agents naturally guess command patterns like:
- `gt witness nudge <polecat>`
- `gt polecat nudge <name>`
- `gt crew restart <member>`

But these don't exist - the actual commands are:
- `gt nudge <session> <message>`
- `gt polecat reset <name>`

This creates friction in agent UX. Agents shouldn't have to memorize the 'correct' command structure.

## Proposal

Add subcommands to each role that delegate to core commands with argument reordering:

```
gt witness nudge furiosa "Start working"
  → gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa "Start working"

gt polecat nudge gastown/furiosa "Check mail"
  → gt nudge gt-gastown-furiosa "Check mail"

gt crew nudge gastown/max "Wake up"
  → gt nudge gt-crew-gastown-max "Wake up"
```

## Benefits

1. **Discoverable**: Agents explore `gt witness --help` and find nudge
2. **Lenient**: Multiple valid ways to express the same intent
3. **Role-contextual**: Commands under the role namespace feel natural
4. **Extensible**: Pattern works for future subcommands (status, reset, etc.)

## Implementation

Each role command (witness, polecat, crew, refinery) gets thin wrapper subcommands:

```go
// In witness.go
witnessCmd.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
    Use:   "nudge <polecat> <message>",
    Short: "Nudge a polecat (delegates to gt nudge)",
    RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
        session := formatSession(rig, args[0])
        return runNudge(session, args[1])
    },
})
```

## Future Candidates

Other subcommands to add as role-specific aliases:
- `gt polecat status <name>` (already exists, good pattern)
- `gt witness check <polecat>` → trigger a manual check
- `gt refinery merge <mr-id>` → process specific MR
- `gt crew send <member> <message>` → mail to crew member","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:05:17.303809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:05:17.303809-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-aqku","title":"BUG: gt done doesn't push branch to origin - work lost when worktree deleted","description":"## Problem

`gt done` creates a merge-request record in beads but **never pushes the branch to origin**.

When polecats are cleaned up (worktrees deleted), the branch is lost forever. The MR record exists but there's no actual branch to merge.

## Evidence

12 MQ items had `ready` status but no corresponding branches on origin. All polecat worktrees were deleted, losing all work.

## Impact

**CRITICAL**: All polecat work is silently lost unless polecats manually push (which they don't know to do).

## Fix

In `done.go`, before creating the MR:
1. `git push origin <branch>` to push the branch to remote
2. Only create MR record if push succeeds

## Location
`mayor/rig/internal/cmd/done.go:124-135`","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:21:27.012045-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:22:45.104108-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:22:45.104108-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in done.go and mq_submit.go - both now push branch before creating MR"}
 {"id":"gt-aqm","title":"Beads as Universal Data Plane","description":"## Vision

Beads is the data plane for all Gas Town operations. Everything flows through beads:
- Work items (issues, tasks, epics)
- Mail (messages between agents)
- Merge requests (queue entries)
- Workflows (composable execution patterns)
- Resources (leases, locks, quotas)
- Schedules (timed activities)

## New Bead Categories

### Molecules (Composable Workflows)
Crystallized workflow patterns that can be attached to work items.

### Timed Beads (Scheduled Work)
Beads that wake up periodically via daemon.

### Pinned Beads (Ongoing Concerns)
Beads representing persistent concerns, not discrete tasks.

### Resource Beads (Leases/Locks)
Beads representing reserved resources.

## v1 Priority

- **P0**: Molecules (enables engineer-in-box)

 {"id":"gt-mh5s","title":"Refinery gates: test/lint/build before merge","description":"Before merging polecat work to main, run configurable quality gates.

**From VC**: internal/gates/ - parallel execution with timeout, any failure = overall failure.

**Gas Town implementation**: Refinery config with gate commands:
```yaml
gates:
  test:
    cmd: go test ./...
    timeout: 5m
  lint:
    cmd: golangci-lint run
    timeout: 2m
  build:
    cmd: go build ./...
    timeout: 3m
parallel: true
```

If gates fail, don't merge. Polecat can iterate and retry.

**Value**: Prevents broken code from reaching main. VC had 90.9% gate pass rate.

**VC complexity**: ~200 lines Go
**Gas Town complexity**: ~10 lines YAML","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:12.44681-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:12.44681-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-mh5s","depends_on_id":"gt-zhpa","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:30:27.402708-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-mx6s","title":"Witness patrol wisp with polecat leases","description":"Witness should maintain a rolling patrol wisp that tracks active polecats:

wisp-witness-patrol

Patrol loop iterates leases:
- gt peek $polecat
- If idle: gt nudge
- If shutdown received: close lease

When all leases closed:
- bd mol squash wisp-xxx --summary='N polecats processed'

+{"id":"gt-qp2w","title":"mol-witness-patrol","description":"Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop using the Christmas Ornament pattern.

The Witness is the Pit Boss for your rig. You watch polecats, nudge them toward
completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## The Christmas Ornament Shape

This molecule uses dynamic bonding to create inspection arms per-polecat:

```
                    ★ mol-witness-patrol (trunk)
                   /|\
            ┌─────┘ │ └─────┐
         PREFLIGHT DISCOVERY CLEANUP
            │       │        │
        ┌───┴───┐ ┌─┴─┐  ┌───┴───┐
        │inbox  │ │sur│  │aggreg │
        │refnry │ │vey│  │save   │
        │load   │ └─┬─┘  │summary│
        └───────┘   │    │contxt │
                    │    │loop   │
          ┌─────────┼─────────┐ └───────┘
          │         │         │
          ●         ●         ●     mol-polecat-arm (dynamic)
         ace       nux      toast
          │         │         │
       ┌──┴──┐   ┌──┴──┐   ┌──┴──┐
       │cap  │   │cap  │   │cap  │
       │ass  │   │ass  │   │ass  │
       │dec  │   │dec  │   │dec  │
       │exec │   │exec │   │exec │
       └──┬──┘   └──┬──┘   └──┬──┘
          │         │         │
          └─────────┴─────────┘
                    │
                 ⬣ base (cleanup)
```

## Phases

### PREFLIGHT (fixed steps)
1. inbox-check - Process lifecycle requests, help messages
2. check-refinery - Ensure MQ is alive and processing
3. load-state - Read persistent state (nudge counts, etc.)

### DISCOVERY (spawns dynamic arms)
4. survey-workers - List polecats, bond mol-polecat-arm per polecat
5. run-plugins - Bond mol-plugin-runner for each witness plugin

### CLEANUP (gate + fixed steps)
6. aggregate - GATE: WaitsFor all arms + plugins to complete
7. save-state - Persist nudge counts, action log
8. generate-summary - Create digest content
9. context-check - Check if context is high
10. burn-or-loop - Squash/burn wisp, then loop or exit

## Dynamic Arms

Each polecat gets mol-polecat-arm bonded as a wisp child:
- capture - Capture tmux output
- assess - Categorize state (working/idle/error/done)
- load-history - Get nudge counts for this polecat
- decide - Apply nudge matrix
- execute - Take action (nudge/kill/escalate/none)

Arms run in PARALLEL. The aggregate step waits for all to complete.

## Activity Feed

This design enables real-time visibility:

```
[14:32:08] + patrol-x7k.arm-ace bonded (5 steps)
[14:32:09] → patrol-x7k.arm-ace.capture in_progress
[14:32:10] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace.capture completed
[14:32:14] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace.decide completed (action: nudge-1)
[14:32:17] ✓ patrol-x7k.arm-ace COMPLETE
```

## Storage

- Wisp storage: .beads-wisp/ (ephemeral, gitignored)
- Persistent state: witness handoff bead (nudge counts, etc.)
- Digests: Squashed summaries in permanent beads

## Dependencies

- bd-xo1o: Dynamic Molecule Bonding epic (in beads rig)
  - bd mol bond with variable substitution
  - WaitsFor directive for fanout gates
  - Activity feed query

Labels: [template, christmas-ornament]","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"stevey","created_at":"2025-12-23T16:18:21.59836-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T16:18:21.614437-08:00","wisp":true}
 {"id":"gt-qp98","title":"Refactor: Eliminate state.json, use beads assignee field for polecat state","description":"## Problem

state.json in each polecat worktree duplicates data that should be in beads:
- `issue` field duplicates assignment (should be issue.assignee)
- `state` field duplicates status (derivable from issue.status)
- `name/rig/branch/clone_path` are all derivable from filesystem/git

This violates 'Beads as the data plane' (HOP Decision 001).

## Current State

```json
{
  "name": "Angharad",        // Derivable: basename(cwd)
  "rig": "gastown",          // Derivable: parent dir name
  "state": "working",        // Should be: issue.status
  "clone_path": "/path",     // Derivable: cwd
  "branch": "polecat/X",     // Derivable: git branch
  "issue": "gt-xyz",         // Should be: issue.assignee
  "timestamps": "..."        // Should be in beads history
}
```

## Target State

**Polecat identity**: Derived from worktree path
**Polecat assignment**: `issue.assignee = 'gastown/Angharad'`
**Polecat state**: Derived from issue.status (in_progress = working, closed = done)
**Polecat existence**: Worktree directory exists

## Benefits

1. Single source of truth (no sync issues)
2. Queryable: `bd list --assignee=gastown/Angharad`
3. History tracking via beads
4. Cross-agent coordination via beads sync
5. Simpler code (no state file management)

## Implementation

1. Update `gt spawn` to set issue.assignee instead of writing state.json
2. Update polecat list/status commands to query beads
3. Update Witness to query beads for polecat state
4. Remove state.json read/write code
5. Keep state.json as optional bootstrap cache (perf optimization only)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T11:40:33.389689-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:43.519059-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T12:07:43.519059-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-sult","title":"gt spawn beads sync warning is misleading for redirect-based polecats","description":"## Problem

When spawning a polecat, gt spawn shows a warning:
```
Warning: beads sync: exit status 1
```

This is misleading because polecats using the redirect architecture (`.beads/redirect`) share the canonical database at `mayor/rig/.beads/beads.db`. The 'stale beads' indicated by the warning is just git branch divergence (main vs beads-sync), not actual data staleness.

## Expected Behavior

gt spawn should either:
1. Skip the beads sync check for polecats using redirects (they share the canonical DB)
2. Or provide a clearer message like 'beads redirect active, using shared database'

## Reproduction

```bash
gt spawn --issue gt-xxx --rig gastown --create
# Shows 'Warning: beads sync: exit status 1' even though beads are current
```

## Root Cause

spawn.go calls beads sync and treats any non-zero exit as a warning. But with redirects, the polecat doesn't need its own beads - it uses the canonical source via the redirect chain:
```
polecat/.beads/redirect -> ../../.beads -> gastown/.beads/redirect -> mayor/rig/.beads
```

## Fix Options

1. Check for .beads/redirect before calling sync
2. Have bd sync return 0 when redirect is present
3. Suppress the warning in spawn when redirect exists","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:02:01.28061-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:02:01.28061-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-w3bu","title":"gt spawn: Enter key needs debounce delay after paste","description":"## Problem

When spawning polecats via `gt spawn`, instructions are pasted into tmux but workers often sit idle at prompt because the Enter key arrives before the paste is fully processed.

## Observed Behavior

- Instructions pasted via tmux send-keys
- Enter key sent immediately after
- Worker session shows prompt with no input (paste not submitted)
- Requires manual intervention to nudge workers

## Expected Behavior

- Paste completes fully
- Enter key submits the pasted instructions
- Worker begins executing immediately

## Root Cause

Likely a race condition between paste buffer processing and keypress handling. Need either:
1. Debounce delay before sending Enter
2. Longer delay (Tmax) to ensure paste completes
3. Alternative submission mechanism

## Impact

- Swarm of 10 workers had multiple stalls at startup
- Required manual tmux send-keys to unstick them
- Defeats purpose of automated spawning

## References

- Observed during Mad Max swarm (Dec 18, 2025)
- Affects gt spawn command in internal/cmd/spawn.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:06:50.734123-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T21:08:22.400899-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-w9o","title":"/restart: Personal slash command for in-place agent restart","description":"Create ~/.claude/commands/restart.md that restarts current Gas Town agent in place.

## Detection
- Read tmux session name: gt-mayor, gt-witness-*, gt-refinery-*, gt-polecat-*
- Fallback: check GT_ROLE env var

## Behavior by role
- mayor: gt mayor restart (sends Ctrl-C, loop respawns)
- witness: gt witness restart
- refinery: gt refinery restart  
- polecat: gt polecat restart (or witness-mediated)

## Command format
Uses backticks for inline bash to detect context, then instructs Claude to run appropriate restart.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:30.043125-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T18:43:17.182303-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-wpg","title":"Replaceable notifications via Claude Code queue","description":"Leverage Claude Code's ability to replace queued text for notifications that supersede previous ones.

## Problem

If daemon sends 10 heartbeats while agent is busy, agent returns to see 10 stacked messages. Wasteful and noisy.

## Solution

Use Claude Code's queue replacement for:
- Heartbeat messages (only latest matters)
- Status updates that supersede previous
- Progress notifications

## Implementation

Notifications get a 'slot' identifier. New notification in same slot replaces old one:
- Slot: 'heartbeat' → only one heartbeat queued at a time
- Slot: 'status-<rig>' → latest status per rig
- No slot → stacks normally (for unique messages)

## Research Needed

- How does Claude Code expose queue replacement?
- tmux send-keys behavior with pending input
- Alternative: clear + resend pattern","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:29.821949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:19:00.398942-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:19:00.398942-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-wpg","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T14:19:46.656972-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
